scope: L group round 2 pre-prediction factual data refresh only; no score prediction, no WDL lean, no betting advice.

evidence: England beat Croatia 4-2 after the local pre-match file had a lower-scoring 1-1 baseline.
England scored through Kane 12 pen, Kane 42, Bellingham 47 and Rashford 85.
Local review and Group L page both record a faster England second-half restart and late substitute contribution from Saka to Rashford.
england_attack_conversion_and_second_hal.data_reminder: Raise attention on England chance conversion, set-piece second phase and post-interval intensity; keep defensive transition risk visible.

evidence: Croatia scored twice through Baturina 36 and Musa 45+5, with Sucic and Perisic assist paths.
Croatia also conceded four and made multiple second-half changes after England regained control.
croatia_transition_threat_but_defense_br.data_reminder: Keep Croatia attacking transition and veteran passing value, but flag defensive-line and wide-space risk after conceding 4.

evidence: Ghana beat Panama 1-0 through Caleb Yirenkyi at 90+5, assisted by Brandon Thomas-Asante from the bench.
Local review notes a low-scoring game state and late direct attack pattern.
ghana_low_score_breakthrough_and_sub_imp.data_reminder: Track Ghana substitute impact, direct counterattacks and late-game scoring path without treating it as a prediction.

evidence: Panama held Ghana scoreless until stoppage time but produced no goal in the opener.
Panama cards recorded locally: Cesar Blackman 72, Carlos Harvey 90+9.
panama_low_block_resistance_limited_atta.data_reminder: Preserve Panama low-block resistance but mark attacking production as limited after round 1.

evidence: Ghana goalkeeper Lawrence Ati-Zigi started vs Panama and was replaced by Benjamin Asare at 46/47 in the local review and Group L page lineups.
No final official Ghana medical bulletin was retrieved in this pass.
ati_zigi_next_match_availability_risk.data_reminder: Mark Ghana goalkeeper availability as a T-24/T-75 recheck item; do not assume full availability.

key_availability_watch: England: Declan Rice trained after hamstring/back discomfort and publicly said he was ready for Ghana; still a load-risk watch item.
England: Bukayo Saka has an Achilles management issue and is reported likely to remain a bench option rather than a full-90 starter.
England: Marcus Rashford returned to training after a minor muscular/tight hamstring concern but remains a minutes-risk watch item.
Ghana: Lawrence Ati-Zigi was substituted at half-time/46 vs Panama; final medical status was not confirmed in this pass.

Ghana: Thomas Partey was unavailable for the Canada-based Panama match due visa/admissibility reporting but Guardian reported he was allowed into the United States for Ghana's Boston and Philadelphia games.
